Stakeholders welcomed the intervention, expressing optimism that the stimulus package would help stabilize economic activities, support household incomes and lay the foundation for a gradual recovery as the country continued responding to the challenges created by the pandemic.

headline: Rising Food Prices Push Nigeria's Inflation Rate Higher
summary: Nigeria recorded a rise in inflation as higher food prices and supply chain disruptions increased the cost of living nationwide.
fullStory: Nigeria experienced a notable increase in inflation during the middle of 2020 as rising food prices, supply chain disruptions and broader economic pressures combined to drive up the cost of essential goods across the country. The higher prices affected household purchasing power, placing additional financial strain on families already facing the economic consequences of the COVID-19 pandemic. Market analysts attributed the upward trend to several factors, including logistical challenges associated with movement restrictions, exchange rate pressures, increased transportation costs and reduced agricultural output in some farming communities. They noted that disruptions to food production and distribution contributed to shortages of key commodities, leading to higher prices in markets nationwide. In response, the Central Bank and other economic policymakers closely monitored inflationary developments while introducing measures aimed at improving food supply chains, supporting agricultural production and maintaining overall macroeconomic stability. Government officials emphasized the importance of boosting domestic food production, strengthening storage and distribution infrastructure and encouraging investment in agriculture to reduce dependence on imports and improve food security. Economists argued that addressing inflation would require coordinated fiscal and monetary policies alongside sustained efforts to increase productivity in critical sectors of the economy. They also stressed that long-term price stability depends on improving infrastructure, reducing production costs and creating a more resilient supply chain capable of withstanding future disruptions. Stakeholders expressed hope that ongoing interventions would gradually ease inflationary pressures and restore greater stability to Nigeria's economy.

headline: Economic Downturn Deepens Challenges for Small Businesses Across Nigeria
summary: Small businesses across Nigeria struggled to remain operational as the prolonged economic downturn reduced consumer spending and disrupted commercial activities.
fullStory: Small businesses across Nigeria continued to face significant economic challenges as the prolonged downturn placed increasing pressure on entrepreneurs, traders and operators in the informal sector. Declining consumer spending, reduced business activity and operational restrictions forced many enterprises to scale down production, reduce their workforce or suspend operations temporarily in an effort to remain financially viable. Business owners reported persistent difficulties in maintaining supply chains, meeting operational expenses and accessing affordable credit needed to sustain their activities during the period of economic uncertainty. Informal sector workers, who rely heavily on daily commercial transactions, also experienced substantial income losses as reduced market activities affected their ability to earn a living. Economic analysts observed that small and medium-sized enterprises remained among the sectors most vulnerable to prolonged economic disruptions because of limited financial reserves and restricted access to formal financing. They noted that strengthening the resilience of small businesses would be critical to protecting employment, supporting domestic production and accelerating economic recovery. Financial experts called on policymakers to expand intervention programmes by improving access to low-interest loans, providing targeted financial relief and creating policies that encourage business growth and investment. They also emphasized the need to simplify lending procedures and improve support services for entrepreneurs seeking to rebuild their businesses. Stakeholders expressed optimism that stronger government interventions, combined with improved access to finance and a more stable economic environment, would enable small businesses to recover, create jobs and continue contributing to Nigeria's long-term economic development.

headline: Central Bank Rolls Out Measures to Stabilise Foreign Exchange Market
summary: The Central Bank of Nigeria introduced new measures aimed at improving foreign exchange stability and protecting the value of the naira.
fullStory: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) introduced a series of policy measures designed to stabilise the country's foreign exchange market as authorities sought to manage growing economic uncertainty and preserve macroeconomic stability. The measures included tighter controls on foreign exchange demand, restrictions on access to official foreign exchange for certain imported goods and additional initiatives aimed at conserving the nation's foreign reserves. According to the apex bank, the policies were intended to protect the value of the naira, reduce pressure on the foreign exchange market and encourage greater reliance on locally produced goods and services. Officials maintained that improving exchange rate stability remained essential to supporting investor confidence, maintaining financial system stability and safeguarding the country's external reserves during a period of global economic challenges. While some stakeholders welcomed the intervention as a necessary response to external market pressures, businesses dependent on imported raw materials and finished products expressed concerns about the potential impact on production costs, supply chains and consumer prices. Economic analysts observed that exchange rate management continues to be one of Nigeria's most significant macroeconomic challenges, particularly for an economy with substantial import demand and fluctuating foreign exchange earnings. They argued that achieving long-term currency stability would require broader structural reforms, increased export diversification, stronger domestic production and sustained growth in non-oil sources of foreign exchange. Analysts also noted that improving investor confidence and expanding local manufacturing would help reduce dependence on imports while strengthening the resilience of the Nigerian economy against future external shocks.

headline: Federal Government Intensifies Agricultural Drive to Strengthen Food Security
summary: The Federal Government has expanded support for agriculture to improve food security and reduce dependence on imported food products.
fullStory: The Federal Government has intensified its focus on agriculture as part of broader efforts to strengthen national food security, reduce dependence on imported food products and stimulate sustainable economic growth. Under the renewed agricultural agenda, authorities introduced several programmes aimed at supporting farmers through improved access to farm inputs, financing opportunities, extension services and stronger market linkages. Government officials explained that increasing domestic agricultural production remains a key strategy for addressing rising food prices, improving rural livelihoods and enhancing the country's resilience against global supply disruptions. They noted that expanding agricultural productivity would not only improve food availability but also create employment opportunities, increase non-oil economic activity and contribute to long-term economic diversification. The administration also reaffirmed its commitment to encouraging private sector investment in agriculture while promoting value addition, agro-processing and modern farming techniques capable of increasing productivity across the sector. Agricultural experts welcomed the renewed emphasis on farming, describing it as a positive step toward improving national food security and reducing inflationary pressures linked to food shortages. However, they stressed that sustained progress would require significant investment in rural infrastructure, irrigation systems, storage facilities, transportation networks and access to affordable financing for farmers. Analysts also observed that strengthening agricultural value chains and improving market efficiency would help minimise post-harvest losses while increasing farmers' incomes. Stakeholders expressed optimism that continued support for the sector would strengthen Nigeria's agricultural competitiveness, improve food affordability and contribute meaningfully to inclusive economic development.

headline: Nigeria's Digital Economy Records Strong Growth Amid Pandemic
summary: Nigeria's digital economy expanded significantly in 2020 as businesses and consumers increasingly embraced online services during the pandemic.
fullStory: Nigeria's digital economy recorded remarkable growth during 2020 as businesses, government institutions and millions of citizens increasingly adopted digital platforms to sustain economic and social activities amid COVID-19 restrictions. The widespread movement limitations introduced to contain the pandemic accelerated the use of e-commerce, digital banking, online education, telemedicine and remote working solutions across the country. Businesses that traditionally relied on physical interactions rapidly expanded their online presence, while financial technology companies experienced increased demand for electronic payment services as consumers shifted toward cashless transactions. The technology sector also witnessed greater investment in digital infrastructure and innovation, with startups introducing new solutions to meet evolving consumer needs during the public health crisis. Government agencies encouraged the use of digital platforms for service delivery, while telecommunications providers expanded network capacity to accommodate rising internet traffic resulting from remote work and virtual communication. Technology analysts described the rapid digital transformation as one of the few positive economic developments to emerge from the pandemic, noting that it accelerated trends that might otherwise have taken several years to materialize. They argued that sustained investment in broadband infrastructure, digital literacy, cybersecurity and innovation-friendly policies would be necessary to maintain the sector's momentum beyond the pandemic. Analysts also observed that the expanding digital economy presents significant opportunities for entrepreneurship, job creation, financial inclusion and economic diversification. Stakeholders expressed optimism that continued growth in digital services would strengthen Nigeria's competitiveness, improve access to financial and commercial opportunities and position the country as one of Africa's leading technology-driven economies.

headline: Federal Government Reviews National Budget Over Revenue Shortfalls
summary: The Federal Government reviewed the national budget to address declining revenues caused by falling oil prices and economic disruptions.
fullStory: The Federal Government conducted a comprehensive review of the national budget following significant revenue shortfalls triggered by declining global oil prices and broader economic disruptions that affected public finances. The review was aimed at realigning government spending with prevailing fiscal realities while ensuring that critical national priorities continued to receive adequate funding. As part of the adjustments, greater emphasis was placed on essential sectors including healthcare, security, social welfare and other strategic areas considered vital to protecting lives and sustaining economic stability during a challenging period. Government officials explained that the revised budget framework was designed to improve fiscal discipline, optimize available resources and maintain the delivery of key public services despite reduced government income. They also reaffirmed the administration's commitment to prudent financial management and the efficient allocation of public funds in line with changing economic conditions. Financial analysts described the budget review as a necessary response to evolving fiscal pressures, noting that flexibility in public expenditure is essential during periods of economic uncertainty. They argued that regular budget assessments enable governments to respond more effectively to external shocks while preserving macroeconomic stability and supporting long-term development objectives. Economists also emphasized the importance of diversifying government revenue sources, strengthening tax administration and promoting non-oil sectors to reduce vulnerability to fluctuations in global commodity prices. Stakeholders expressed optimism that sound fiscal management and continued economic reforms would enhance Nigeria's ability to navigate future economic challenges while laying the foundation for sustainable growth and improved public service delivery.

headline: Economic Recovery Plans Target Sustainable Post-Pandemic Growth
summary: Economic policymakers unveiled recovery proposals aimed at accelerating growth and strengthening Nigeria's post-pandemic economy.
fullStory: As 2020 drew to a close, economic policymakers intensified efforts to chart a path toward sustainable recovery following the severe economic disruption caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. Government officials and financial experts proposed a range of policy measures designed to restore economic stability, stimulate investment and create employment opportunities across key sectors of the economy. The recovery framework placed significant emphasis on accelerating infrastructure development, expanding support for productive industries and diversifying government revenue sources beyond crude oil. Policymakers argued that increased investment in transportation, energy, agriculture, manufacturing and the digital economy would improve productivity while creating new opportunities for businesses and young Nigerians. The proposals also highlighted the importance of strengthening fiscal management, improving the business environment and encouraging greater private sector participation in national development. Economists welcomed the recovery plans, describing them as an important foundation for rebuilding economic confidence after one of the country's most challenging economic periods. However, they stressed that achieving lasting recovery would require consistent policy implementation, institutional reforms and transparent governance capable of sustaining investor confidence. Analysts further observed that structural reforms, improved infrastructure and continued economic diversification would be critical to reducing future vulnerability to external shocks while supporting inclusive growth. Stakeholders expressed optimism that coordinated implementation of the proposed measures would accelerate economic recovery, improve employment opportunities and position Nigeria for stronger and more resilient long-term development.

headline: Nigeria's Economy Shows Early Signs of Recovery After Pandemic Shock
summary: Nigeria's economy began a gradual recovery in 2021 as business activities resumed and government stimulus measures supported economic growth.
fullStory: Nigeria's economy recorded early signs of recovery in 2021 following the severe disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, with economic activities gradually resuming across major sectors as restrictions were eased and businesses adapted to changing operating conditions. Commercial activities improved as manufacturers, retailers, financial institutions and service providers adjusted to the post-pandemic environment while consumer confidence slowly strengthened. The Federal Government continued implementing economic stimulus measures and targeted spending programmes aimed at supporting businesses, protecting jobs and encouraging investment in strategic sectors of the economy. Authorities also expanded interventions for small and medium-sized enterprises, recognising their critical role in employment generation and domestic production. Economic analysts observed that although growth had returned, the recovery remained uneven across sectors, with some industries recovering faster than others due to differences in demand, financing and operational capacity. They noted that maintaining macroeconomic stability, improving infrastructure and strengthening investor confidence would be essential to sustaining the recovery over the medium term. Economists also stressed the importance of increasing productivity, diversifying the economy beyond oil and implementing structural reforms capable of improving competitiveness and resilience. They argued that continued investment in agriculture, manufacturing, technology and infrastructure would help create jobs while supporting long-term economic expansion. Stakeholders expressed cautious optimism that consistent policy implementation, prudent fiscal management and stronger collaboration between the public and private sectors would consolidate the recovery and place Nigeria on a more sustainable path of economic growth.

headline: Rising Inflation Continues to Increase Cost of Living Across Nigeria
summary: Persistent inflation and rising food prices placed greater financial pressure on households across Nigeria in early 2021.
fullStory: Nigeria continued to experience sustained inflationary pressure in early 2021 as rising food prices and broader economic challenges pushed the cost of living higher across the country. The prices of essential commodities including rice, bread, cooking oil and other staple food items increased in several markets, placing additional financial strain on households already recovering from the economic impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. Economists attributed the persistent inflation to a combination of supply chain disruptions, insecurity affecting agricultural production in key farming communities, exchange rate pressures and rising transportation costs. They observed that reduced farm output and logistical difficulties contributed to shortages in food supply, further driving up market prices nationwide. The increasing cost of living became a major concern for consumers, businesses and policymakers as purchasing power declined and household budgets came under greater pressure. In response, government authorities announced ongoing efforts to improve food production, strengthen distribution networks and implement policies aimed at stabilising commodity prices. Officials emphasized the importance of supporting local agriculture, improving infrastructure and addressing supply bottlenecks to increase food availability and reduce inflationary pressures over time. Analysts argued that achieving sustainable price stability would require coordinated fiscal and monetary policies, stronger investment in agriculture and continued economic reforms capable of improving productivity across key sectors. Stakeholders expressed hope that the combined policy measures would gradually moderate inflation, strengthen food security and improve living conditions as Nigeria's economic recovery gathered momentum.

headline: Central Bank Tightens Monetary Policy to Curb Inflation
summary: The Central Bank introduced tighter monetary measures aimed at controlling inflation and strengthening macroeconomic stability.
fullStory: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) introduced tighter monetary policy measures as part of efforts to curb rising inflation, strengthen the value of the national currency and maintain overall macroeconomic stability. The policy adjustments included changes to key interest rates, enhanced liquidity management strategies and other regulatory measures designed to moderate money supply within the economy. According to officials of the apex bank, the interventions were intended to reduce inflationary pressures, preserve price stability and create a more predictable economic environment capable of supporting long-term growth. They maintained that stable monetary conditions remain essential for attracting investment, protecting purchasing power and strengthening confidence in Nigeria's financial system. While the measures received support from some financial experts who viewed them as necessary to address persistent inflation, others expressed concern that tighter monetary conditions could increase borrowing costs and limit access to credit, particularly for small and medium-sized enterprises that rely heavily on bank financing. Economists noted that balancing inflation control with economic expansion remains one of the most significant policy challenges facing developing economies. They argued that monetary tightening alone would not be sufficient to achieve lasting stability without complementary fiscal reforms, improved domestic production and stronger infrastructure development. Analysts further observed that increasing productivity, encouraging investment and expanding non-oil sectors would help strengthen the economy while reducing inflationary risks over time. Stakeholders expressed optimism that coordinated policy implementation would support sustainable economic growth while gradually restoring greater price and financial stability across the country.

headline: CONUA Backs NAMDA’s Right to Industrial Action, Warns Against Alleged Intimidation of Members Over CATA
summary: CONUA has backed NAMDA’s right to lawful industrial action while alleging attempts to intimidate its members over the payment of the Consolidated Academic Tools Allowance (CATA).
fullStory: The Congress of University Academics (CONUA) has expressed solidarity with the National Association of Medical and Dental Academics (NAMDA) over its position on the ongoing renegotiation of the 2009 Federal Government–University-Based Unions Agreement, affirming that every registered trade union has the legal right to pursue lawful industrial action in defence of its members. In a statement issued on Thursday and signed by its National President, Dr. Niyi Sunmonu, CONUA said it had taken note of NAMDA's readiness to consider industrial action should the ongoing renegotiation fail to produce satisfactory outcomes. The union maintained that while the proposed Joint Action Front (JAF) involving university-based unions is still being formalised, it supports NAMDA's demand for the timely conclusion of the renegotiation process and equitable treatment of all recognised unions within the university system. CONUA called on the Chairman of the Renegotiation Committee, Alhaji Yayale Ahmed, to conclude the assignment without further delay and invite all university-based unions to sign their respective agreements. It also expressed confidence in the Minister of Education, Dr. Tunji Alausa, to sustain what it described as his commitment to fairness, inclusiveness and equal treatment of all unions participating in the renegotiation process. Beyond the renegotiation, the union raised concerns over what it described as attempts by the leadership of the Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU) to intimidate its members regarding the payment of the Consolidated Academic Tools Allowance (CATA). CONUA alleged that some of its members had been told they were ineligible for the allowance because of their affiliation with the union, describing such claims as false, divisive and contrary to the principles of fairness. According to the union, it had already reached an understanding in principle with the Yayale Ahmed-led committee on the implementation of approved CATA adjustments pending the formal signing of its agreement with the Federal Government. It therefore insisted that no member should be denied any approved benefit because of union affiliation and urged Vice-Chancellors to comply fully with the Federal Ministry of Education's directives by implementing approved allowances and policies fairly and without discrimination. Reaffirming its commitment to industrial harmony and constructive engagement, CONUA stated that while it remains open to dialogue, the welfare, dignity and legitimate rights of its members remain non-negotiable and would be defended through every lawful means as it continues to advocate reforms that strengthen Nigeria's university system.

headline: Group Condemns Alleged Threat by Ex-Governor Yahaya Bello Against Opposition Leaders
summary: A peace advocacy group has condemned remarks allegedly made by former Kogi State Governor Yahaya Bello against opposition leaders, describing them as a threat to democratic governance.
fullStory: A peace advocacy group in Kogi Central, Justice and Order, Say No to Violence in Ebira Land, has condemned what it described as inflammatory remarks allegedly made by former Kogi State Governor Yahaya Bello, in which he reportedly called for the arrest and prosecution of opposition leaders whom he referred to as 'political criminals.' In a statement signed by its spokesperson, Alhaji Oganya Abdurazak Mamman, the group said the alleged remarks were undemocratic and posed a serious threat to democratic governance, civil liberties and the rule of law. According to the group, democracy can only flourish where there is a vibrant opposition capable of holding those in power accountable, promoting transparency and providing alternative ideas for governance and national development. Oganya said it was not the first time Bello had allegedly made statements perceived as threatening toward political opponents, recalling similar comments made ahead of the 2023 general elections. The group expressed concern that with another election cycle approaching, Bello was again allegedly encouraging the current administration to take action against opposition figures under the pretext of dealing with what he described as 'political criminals.' It argued that such rhetoric was reckless, inflammatory and capable of encouraging political persecution, intimidation and violence if left unchecked. The organisation therefore called on the Inspector-General of Police, the Department of State Services, the Attorney-General of the Federation, the judiciary and President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to treat the alleged comments with the seriousness they deserve. It further noted that President Tinubu himself was once a prominent opposition leader and therefore understands the vital role opposition parties play in sustaining democracy and protecting political pluralism. According to the statement, Bello allegedly made the remarks in Abuja during the Call to Bar ceremony of the daughter of the Kogi State Accountant-General and APC chieftain, Hajia Habibat Tijani Onumoko. Reaffirming its commitment to peaceful democratic engagement, the group maintained that Nigeria's democracy must never become a system where dissenting opinions are criminalised or political opponents are intimidated. It insisted that every Nigerian has a constitutional right to participate freely in the democratic process without fear of harassment, victimisation or political persecution.

headline: FG Arrests Two Suspected Illegal Miners in Osun, Intensifies Nationwide Crackdown
summary: The Federal Government has intensified its crackdown on illegal mining following the arrest of two suspected illegal miners during a joint enforcement operation in Osun State.
fullStory: The Federal Government has intensified its nationwide crackdown on illegal mining following the arrest of two suspected illegal miners during a coordinated enforcement operation at an illegal mining site in Osun State. The operation was carried out by officials of the Ministry of Solid Minerals Development from its Ogun, Oyo and Osun State offices in collaboration with security agencies. The enforcement team targeted illegal artisanal mining camps located at Ileki-Ijesa along the Ile-Ife–Ilesa Road after acting on credible intelligence regarding unlawful mining activities in the area. According to a statement issued by the Special Assistant on Media to the Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Lara Owoeye-Wise, several suspects fled into nearby bushes upon sighting the joint task force, while two individuals were arrested during the operation. The team also recovered mining equipment and dismantled operational bases established by the suspected illegal miners before sealing off the site in accordance with the law. Recovered items included motorcycles, pumping machines, Lister generators, washing equipment, large hose pipes, gold-trapping carpets, cutlasses and other implements allegedly used for illegal mining. Speaking during the operation, the Southwest Zonal Mines Officer, Mr. Ajibade Ganiyu, said the exercise formed part of the Federal Government's sustained efforts to enforce compliance with the Nigerian Minerals and Mining Act, protect the environment and preserve the country's mineral resources. He reaffirmed the ministry's commitment to continuous surveillance and enforcement operations aimed at eliminating illegal mining activities across the country. The Osun State Mines Officer, Mr. Wasiu Adeboye, also warned that sponsors, financiers and operators of illegal mining would continue to face the full weight of the law as enforcement efforts intensify. Reacting to the operation, the Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Mr. Dele Alake, said the Tinubu administration has prioritised the sanitisation of the mining sector through stronger enforcement, regulatory reforms, intelligence gathering and community engagement. He disclosed that the establishment of the Mining Marshals has led to the arrest of more than 300 illegal miners and the prosecution of over 150 suspects, including foreign nationals, while numerous illegal mining sites have been shut down nationwide. The minister urged traditional rulers, community leaders and residents of mining communities to support government efforts by reporting illegal activities, stressing that illegal mining undermines national revenue, fuels insecurity and damages the environment. He assured Nigerians that intelligence-led enforcement operations would continue across the country as part of broader reforms aimed at repositioning the mining sector as a key driver of sustainable economic growth.

headline: Governor Ododo to Attend 7th OJN Annual Symposium, Reaffirms Commitment to Youth Development
summary: Governor Ahmed Usman Ododo will attend the 7th OJN Annual Symposium and Book Launch, reinforcing his administration's commitment to youth development and inclusive governance.
fullStory: Kogi State Governor, Alhaji Ahmed Usman Ododo, is set to attend the 7th OJN Annual Symposium and Book Launch as the Special Guest of Honour, in what organisers describe as a demonstration of the administration's commitment to youth development, leadership and inclusive governance. The symposium, organised by Oladele John Nihi, Special Adviser to the Governor on Youth and Student Matters and Founder of the OJN Foundation, is scheduled to hold on Wednesday, July 15, 2026, at the Kay Galaxy Event Centre in Lokoja. This year's event is themed, 'Youth as Stakeholders in Governance and Democratic Development,' and is expected to bring together policymakers, government officials, development partners, private sector leaders, civil society organisations, students and young professionals to discuss strategies for strengthening youth participation in governance and national development. Organisers said Governor Ododo's participation reflects his administration's continued investment in programmes aimed at empowering young people and expanding opportunities for youth engagement across Kogi State. A major highlight of the event will be the launch of the book, 'Voice of a Generation: The Still Rising Story of Oladele John Nihi.' Proceeds from the book launch will support the activities of the OJN Foundation, including youth empowerment initiatives, educational interventions, leadership development programmes and assistance to vulnerable members of society. The symposium will also feature keynote presentations, panel discussions, goodwill messages from distinguished personalities and the recognition of individuals who have made significant contributions to youth development. Speaking ahead of the event, the Convener, Oladele John Nihi, expressed appreciation to Governor Ododo for accepting the invitation and endorsing the initiative. According to him, the governor's willingness to personally attend the symposium demonstrates his confidence in the capacity of young people and his administration's commitment to creating platforms that promote leadership, innovation and responsible citizenship. He called on youths, students, development partners, stakeholders and members of the public to participate in the symposium, describing it as an opportunity to shape conversations on youth participation in governance while supporting the vision of the OJN Foundation. Organisers added that the event is expected to attract more than 1,500 participants from within and outside Kogi State, making it one of the largest youth-focused gatherings in the state and providing opportunities for dialogue, strategic networking and renewed commitment to developing responsible and transformational leaders.

headline: Adebutu Camp Expresses Confidence Ahead of 2027, Says Ogun PDP Rejecting 'Politics of Greed'
summary: Supporters of Hon. Oladipupo Adebutu say the Ogun PDP is repositioning itself around grassroots mobilisation and disciplined leadership ahead of the 2027 governorship election.
fullStory: Supporters of Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) leader in Ogun State, Hon. Oladipupo Adebutu, have expressed optimism over the party's preparations for the 2027 governorship election, insisting that the PDP is repositioning itself around disciplined leadership, grassroots mobilisation and service-oriented politics. In an opinion article by Adebayo Osonuga, the Adebutu camp argued that the party has moved away from what it described as internal divisions, political sabotage and self-serving interests, replacing them with a renewed commitment to unity, competence and purposeful leadership. According to the article, Adebutu, popularly known as Ladi, represents a new direction for the party, built on sustained engagement with grassroots supporters, strengthened party structures and efforts to restore public confidence in the PDP. The article maintained that Adebutu's years of support for the party and his empowerment initiatives for women and young people demonstrate that his political message extends beyond campaign rhetoric to practical engagement with communities. Supporters also argued that the emerging political culture within the party places greater emphasis on direct service delivery, accountability and grassroots participation rather than patronage politics. Across Ogun State's three senatorial districts, the article claimed that Adebutu has consistently invested in strengthening the PDP by supporting party activities, building alliances and maintaining close contact with party members despite political challenges. It further suggested that this consistency has enabled the former governorship candidate to consolidate support within the party ahead of the next electoral cycle. Reacting to recent defections, the article dismissed concerns that the departure of some members could weaken the PDP, describing such developments as insignificant compared to what it characterised as growing support for Adebutu's leadership across the state. Looking ahead to the 2027 elections, the article projected confidence that the PDP is assembling a disciplined and capable political team focused on reclaiming power in Ogun State. According to the author, supporters believe that voters will ultimately reward integrity, consistency and grassroots leadership over politics driven by personal interests. This report is based on an opinion article and reflects the views expressed by the author and supporters of Hon. Oladipupo Adebutu.

headline: Ex-APC Chieftain Lasem Says He Has No Regrets Joining PDP, Rejects Yayi's Governorship Bid
summary: Former APC chieftain Olanrewaju Sowunmi, popularly known as Lasem, says he has no regrets joining the PDP and insists he cannot support Senator Solomon Olamilekan Adeola's governorship ambition.
fullStory: A former chieftain of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Hon. Olanrewaju Ebenezer Sowunmi, popularly known as Lasem, has defended his decision to defect to the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), saying he has no regrets over leaving the ruling party. Speaking with journalists in Abeokuta, Lasem said his decision was based on principle and his conviction about the political future of Ogun State. According to him, he could not support the governorship ambition of Senator Solomon Olamilekan Adeola, popularly known as Yayi, whom he described as being from Ekiti State. He maintained that his political position was informed by what he described as the need to protect the political interests of Ogun State and ensure that the state's leadership reflects its history, identity and developmental aspirations. Lasem argued that leadership should be entrusted to individuals with a strong understanding of the state's political landscape, cultural heritage and developmental priorities. He added that his decision to join the PDP was motivated by his belief that the party offers a better platform for advancing the interests of Ogun residents ahead of the 2027 governorship election. The former APC stalwart also expressed confidence in the PDP's chances in the forthcoming poll, describing the opposition party as a credible alternative capable of providing purposeful leadership and responsive governance. He urged voters to support candidates they consider competent, visionary and genuinely committed to the progress of Ogun State rather than making decisions based on political affiliations alone. Lasem's comments come amid increasing political consultations, defections and realignments as parties and aspirants begin positioning themselves for the 2027 general elections. His remarks have added to the growing debate surrounding succession politics in Ogun State, where political stakeholders continue to weigh competing interests and potential candidates ahead of the next governorship contest.

headline: Akwa Ibom Assembly Yet to Confirm Alleged Bill on Relationships With Married Men
summary: The Akwa Ibom State House of Assembly is yet to confirm reports claiming it is considering a bill criminalising relationships between unmarried women and married men.
fullStory: Reports claiming that the Akwa Ibom State House of Assembly is considering legislation to criminalise relationships between unmarried women and married men have continued to generate public reactions across social media and other platforms. The reports allege that the proposed bill would prescribe a 10-year prison sentence for unmarried women found to be engaged in sexual relationships with married men, while the married men involved would reportedly be liable to pay a fine. According to the circulating claims, the proposed legislation has been linked to the Deputy Governor of Akwa Ibom State, Senator Akon Eyakenyi. However, the existence, title, sponsorship and legislative status of the alleged bill could not be independently verified at the time of filing this report. Checks did not confirm whether the proposed legislation has been formally introduced before the Akwa Ibom State House of Assembly or whether it has passed any stage of the legislative process. Similarly, there has been no official statement from the Akwa Ibom State House of Assembly confirming the reports or providing details of the purported bill. The Office of the Deputy Governor has also not issued any formal clarification regarding the claims as of the time of filing this report. The reports have nevertheless sparked widespread public debate, with many Nigerians expressing divergent views over the legality, practicality and constitutional implications of such a proposal if eventually introduced. Observers say further clarification from the state legislature and the Deputy Governor's office will be necessary to establish the authenticity of the reports and provide the public with accurate information on the matter. This newspaper will continue to monitor developments and provide updates as more official information becomes available.

headline: Alake Named Tourism Ambassador as NTDA Seeks Partnership on Geo-Tourism Development
summary: The Nigerian Tourism Development Authority has named Solid Minerals Minister Henry Dele Alake a Tourism Ambassador while unveiling plans for collaboration on a national geo-tourism initiative.
fullStory: The Director-General of the Nigerian Tourism Development Authority (NTDA), Mr. Ola Awakan, has decorated the Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Mr. Henry Dele Alake, as a Tourism Ambassador in recognition of his contributions to promoting Nigeria's development potential. The decoration took place during a courtesy visit to the Minister in Abuja, where officials of the NTDA and the Ministry of Solid Minerals Development explored areas of strategic collaboration between the two government institutions. A major focus of the meeting was the development of a comprehensive Geo-tourism Plan designed to harness Nigeria's abundant mineral resources alongside its tourism assets to stimulate economic growth and diversify national revenue. The proposed collaboration is expected to promote the country's unique geological sites as tourism destinations while encouraging sustainable investment in both the mining and tourism sectors. The initiative also seeks to showcase Nigeria's rich geological heritage, create employment opportunities, attract local and foreign visitors, and support the Federal Government's broader economic diversification agenda. Speaking during the visit, both parties reaffirmed their commitment to working together to develop innovative policies and programmes capable of unlocking the economic potential of Nigeria's mineral and tourism industries. They also stressed the importance of strengthening cooperation between government agencies to promote sustainable resource management and increase public awareness of Nigeria's tourism and mining potential. The partnership is expected to position geo-tourism as an emerging driver of sustainable development, investment, cultural promotion and job creation while enhancing Nigeria's profile as a destination for both tourism and responsible mineral exploration.

headline: GSN Hails Tinubu’s Approval of Mining University, Says Move Will Boost Nigeria’s Critical Minerals Sector
summary: The Geological Society of Nigeria says the new Federal University of Mining, Engineering and Technology will strengthen Nigeria's mining sector and develop skilled professionals for the critical minerals industry.
fullStory: The Geological Society of Nigeria (GSN) has applauded President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for approving the upgrade of the Federal Polytechnic, Nasarawa, to the Federal University of Mining, Engineering and Technology, describing the decision as a transformative milestone for Nigeria's solid minerals sector and a strategic investment in the nation's economic future. In a statement issued by its President, Uba Saidu Malami, and made available through the society's Public Relations Officer, Saratu Aliyu, the GSN said the establishment of the specialised university would significantly strengthen Nigeria's capacity to compete in the rapidly expanding global critical minerals market and support the country's participation in the global energy transition. According to the society, the new institution aligns Nigeria's educational system with the demands of the modern economy by focusing on mining, engineering, renewable energy technologies and advanced manufacturing. Malami noted that the approval comes at a time when countries are intensifying efforts to secure supplies of strategic minerals such as lithium, cobalt and rare earth elements required for electric vehicles, solar panels and wind turbines. The society said the initiative reflects the Federal Government's commitment to industrialisation, economic diversification and job creation through specialised education. It also commended the Minister of Solid Minerals Development for providing policy direction that has repositioned the mining sector to attract investment and encourage value addition, while acknowledging the contributions of the Nasarawa State Government and Senator Ahmed Wadada Aliyu in advancing the proposal. According to the GSN, the Federal Ministry of Education, the National Universities Commission and other relevant agencies have been directed to begin implementing the institution's transition into a specialised federal university. The society observed that Nigeria's vast deposits of lithium, tin, columbite, barite and rare earth elements place the country in a strong position to benefit from growing global demand for critical minerals if properly developed. It expressed confidence that the university would become a centre of excellence for training geoscientists, mining engineers, metallurgists and other specialists while promoting research, innovation, responsible mining practices and value addition. The GSN further pledged its support for the institution through curriculum development, accreditation, professional certification and industry collaboration, expressing optimism that the university would play a vital role in developing the skilled workforce needed to unlock Nigeria's mineral wealth and drive sustainable economic growth.

headline: CNG Condemns Exclusion of North-West from ₦3.9 Trillion Federal Road Projects, Demands Immediate Review
summary: The Coalition of Northern Groups has criticised the exclusion of North-West states from recently approved federal road projects, calling for an immediate review of the allocations.
fullStory: The Coalition of Northern Groups (CNG) has expressed strong dissatisfaction with the Federal Government over the exclusion of the seven North-West states from recently approved federal road projects worth more than ₦3.9 trillion, describing the decision as unjust, unacceptable and a betrayal of the region's political support for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu in the 2023 general election. In a statement signed by its National Coordinator, Comrade Jamilu Aliyu Charanchi, the coalition said the omission of Kaduna, Kano, Katsina, Jigawa, Kebbi, Sokoto and Zamfara from the list of beneficiaries reflected what it described as a disturbing pattern of neglect against one of Nigeria's most strategic geopolitical zones. According to the CNG, the North-West made a significant contribution to President Tinubu's electoral victory by delivering millions of votes and mobilising political support during the 2023 presidential election. The coalition argued that residents expected the Federal Government to prioritise projects capable of stimulating economic growth and improving transportation across the region but were instead met with complete exclusion from one of the largest federal infrastructure approvals under the current administration. It noted that major highways such as the Kano–Katsina–Maradi route, Sokoto–Gusau–Funtua corridor and Kaduna–Birnin Gwari road remain critical to agriculture, commerce, security and cross-border trade, yet none featured in the latest approvals. The CNG also expressed concern over the continued delay in completing the Abuja–Kaduna–Kano Expressway, describing the project as vital to national economic activity and public safety. It maintained that approving new multi-billion-naira road projects elsewhere while such strategic infrastructure remains unfinished undermines the Federal Government's commitment to balanced national development. The coalition further criticised the silence of elected and appointed officials from the North-West, including senior lawmakers and ministers, arguing that they have a responsibility to defend the interests of the people they represent. It warned that continued inaction would deepen perceptions of regional marginalisation and called for an immediate review of the approved projects to include critical highways across the seven North-West states. The CNG also urged the Federal Government to accelerate work on the Abuja–Kaduna–Kano Expressway and announce additional interventions for neglected roads across the region, insisting that the North-West deserves infrastructure that reflects its economic importance and contribution to Nigeria's democracy.

headline: Analyst Urges EFCC to Prioritise Evidence Over Media Narratives in Corruption Cases
summary: A public affairs analyst has urged the EFCC to base corruption investigations on credible evidence rather than media narratives to strengthen the administration of justice.
fullStory: A public affairs analyst, Dr. Ephriam Attah, has urged the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) to ensure its investigations are driven by verifiable evidence rather than media narratives, saying corruption cases should be built on facts capable of withstanding judicial scrutiny. Attah made the call while reacting to reports that former Minister of Petroleum Resources, Diezani Alison-Madueke, was acquitted of bribery charges by a court in the United Kingdom. According to him, the outcome of the proceedings underscores the need for anti-corruption agencies to conduct thorough investigations before initiating prosecutions. He argued that allegations alone should never be treated as proof of guilt and stressed that institutions must rely on credible evidence instead of public opinion or media coverage. Attah maintained that the fight against corruption remains essential but must always be anchored on due process, fairness and the rule of law. He said reports indicating that Alison-Madueke was cleared of six bribery-related charges at Southwark Crown Court should encourage Nigerian anti-graft agencies to strengthen the quality of their investigations and ensure prosecutions are supported by compelling evidence. The analyst also expressed concern over the reputational damage that corruption allegations can inflict on public officials before legal proceedings are concluded. Citing former Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation executive Bernard Otti, he noted that individuals linked to corruption investigations often continue to face public criticism regardless of the eventual outcome of judicial processes. According to Attah, Otti has remained associated with allegations connected to the administration of Alison-Madueke despite the principle that every allegation should be tested through due legal process. He stressed that his comments were not intended to weaken anti-corruption efforts but to encourage stronger investigative standards capable of delivering justice while protecting the rights of all parties. Turning to Nigeria's economic reform agenda, Attah argued that the country should place greater value on preserving institutional knowledge within strategic public institutions, particularly in the oil and gas sector. He said experienced professionals such as former NNPC Group Executive Director of Finance and Accounts, Bernard Otti, possess decades of expertise in budget management, revenue reconciliation, audit compliance and financial oversight that could support ongoing reforms aimed at improving transparency and strengthening public financial management. Attah added that Nigeria would benefit from creating structured avenues for retired technocrats to contribute through advisory councils, reform committees and fiscal governance panels, insisting that preserving institutional memory would complement innovation and strengthen the long-term implementation of government policies.

headline: SDP Tells INEC to Ignore Gabam's High Court Judgement, Cites Supreme Court Ruling
summary: The Social Democratic Party has urged INEC to disregard a Federal High Court judgment cited by its expelled former National Chairman, Shehu Musa Gabam, arguing that a subsisting Supreme Court ruling remains binding.
fullStory: The Social Democratic Party (SDP) has urged the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to disregard a Federal High Court judgment being relied upon by its expelled former National Chairman, Shehu Musa Gabam, insisting that the decision cannot supersede an existing Supreme Court ruling on the party's leadership. The party made the position known in a statement issued by its National Publicity Secretary, Araba Rufus Aiyenigba, following a letter reportedly submitted to INEC on Gabam's behalf by White Chambers & Co., forwarding the judgment in Suit No. FHC/ABJ/CS/402/2026. According to the SDP, the Federal High Court judgment is already being challenged before the Court of Appeal and therefore should not form the basis for any action by the electoral commission. The party argued that the plaintiff in the case, Salawu Adeniyi Mustapha Adeoti, was neither its governorship candidate in Osun State nor a participant in its governorship primary, maintaining that Dr. Ajala remains the party's duly nominated candidate. The SDP further contended that the Supreme Court had already settled the issue of party leadership by affirming that courts lack jurisdiction to determine who occupies positions such as National Chairman and National Secretary within political parties. It also maintained that the Federal High Court judgment did not direct INEC to recognise Gabam as National Chairman or Ogbonna Okechukwu as National Secretary. The party argued that aspects of the ruling relied on an earlier Court of Appeal decision that had already been set aside by the Supreme Court before the High Court delivered its judgment. It therefore urged INEC to maintain the status quo pending the final determination of the appeal, warning that recognising Gabam based on the lower court's ruling would amount to allowing a lower court to overrule the Supreme Court. The SDP also accused Gabam of undermining internal democracy, insisting that the party belongs to its members and not to any individual. It said its recent National Executive Committee meeting marked a new phase of constitutional compliance and institutional reforms. The party further disclosed that it had completed the nomination of Prince Adewole Adebayo as its presidential candidate and Dr. Usman Bugaje as his running mate for the 2027 general elections, while preparations continued for the submission of details of other candidates who emerged from its primaries. It added that it remains focused on strengthening party structures, expanding membership, promoting internal democracy and enhancing accountability ahead of the 2027 polls.

headline: Northern Groups Condemn Alleged Exclusion of North-West From ₦3.9tn Federal Road Projects
summary: The Coalition of Northern Groups has criticised the alleged exclusion of the North-West from recently approved federal road projects worth over ₦3.9 trillion, calling for an immediate review.
fullStory: The Coalition of Northern Groups (CNG) has criticised the Federal Government over the alleged exclusion of the North-West geopolitical zone from recently approved federal road projects valued at more than ₦3.9 trillion, describing the development as unfair and inconsistent with the principles of balanced national development. In a statement signed by its National Coordinator, Comrade Jamilu Aliyu Charanchi, the coalition argued that the omission could not be dismissed as an administrative oversight but reflected what it described as a growing pattern of neglect against the region. According to the CNG, the North-West made significant political contributions to the emergence of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu during the 2023 general election and therefore expected equitable consideration in the allocation of major infrastructure projects. The statement noted that the region is home to some of Nigeria's busiest and most economically strategic highways, including the Kano–Katsina–Maradi corridor, the Sokoto–Gusau–Funtua axis and the Kaduna–Birnin Gwari route, all of which play critical roles in agriculture, commerce, manufacturing and national security. The coalition also expressed concern over the continued delay in completing the Abuja–Kaduna–Kano Expressway, saying motorists still face security threats, traffic congestion, accidents and economic losses while new multi-billion-naira road projects are being approved elsewhere. CNG maintained that excluding the North-West from the latest approvals undermines the Federal Government's commitment to fairness, inclusion and equitable distribution of national resources. The group also criticised political office holders from the region for remaining silent, urging them to defend the interests of their constituents. It called on the Federal Government to immediately review the list of approved projects to include critical highways across the seven North-West states and accelerate work on the Abuja–Kaduna–Kano Expressway. Reaffirming its commitment to justice and equal treatment for all regions, the coalition said it would continue to oppose every form of regional marginalisation while advocating infrastructure development that reflects the North-West's contributions to Nigeria's economy and democracy.

headline: FG Breaks 46-Year Industrialisation Jinx as ASCO, NNPCL Seal 20-Year Gas Supply Deal
summary: Ajaokuta Steel Company and the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited have signed a 20-year gas supply agreement expected to accelerate the revival of Nigeria's steel industry.
fullStory: Ajaokuta Steel Company (ASCO) and the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited (NNPCL) have signed a 20-year gas supply agreement in what has been described as a major milestone in Nigeria's long-awaited industrialisation drive. The agreement, executed through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) in Lokoja, Kogi State, is expected to provide uninterrupted gas supply to the Ajaokuta Steel Plant for the next two decades, addressing one of the most significant challenges that has delayed the commencement of steel production for decades. The Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Ajaokuta Steel Company, Prof. Nasir Abdulsalam, described the development as a critical breakthrough capable of unlocking the full potential of the steel complex. Since its establishment more than four decades ago, the Ajaokuta Steel Plant has remained largely inactive despite repeated efforts by successive administrations to revive the project. Industry stakeholders have consistently identified reliable gas supply as one of the essential requirements for attracting investors and completing the operationalisation of the facility. Analysts believe the latest agreement effectively removes that obstacle and could mark the beginning of a new phase in Nigeria's industrial development. The development is also seen as aligning with the Federal Government's economic diversification agenda by strengthening local manufacturing, reducing dependence on imported steel products and encouraging investment in heavy industries. Experts say the revival of Ajaokuta Steel has the potential to create thousands of direct and indirect jobs, stimulate growth in supporting industries and enhance Nigeria's manufacturing capacity. Increased investor confidence is also expected as outstanding technical and commercial processes move closer to completion. Many observers have described the agreement as ending a 46-year delay that has frustrated Nigeria's ambition of establishing a fully functional integrated steel industry. They expressed optimism that the partnership between ASCO and NNPCL would provide the foundation for sustained industrial growth and position the steel plant as a strategic contributor to long-term national economic development.
